\section{IMPORTANT SAFETY INFORMATION}

\safetywarning{INSTRUCTIONS PERTAINING TO RISK OF FIRE, ELECTRIC SHOCK, OR INJURY TO PERSONS}

\begin{safetytwocol}

\safetylead{Always follow these basic precautions when using this product.}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Read all the instructions before using the product.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Do not allow children to play on the product. Close supervision of children is necessary when the product is used near children.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Avoid placing hands or fingers inside the product.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Stop using the product immediately if it has been physically damaged or modified. Improper use may cause unpredictable behavior, leading to fire, explosion, or injury.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
If the product overheats, emits unusual odors or smoke, leaks, or burns, stop using it immediately and contact customer support.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Never attempt to open, repair, or modify the product. Any tampering may result in electric shock, fire, or battery damage.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Be aware that liquid ejected from the product may cause irritation or burns. Avoid contact with leaking liquid and seek medical help if it contacts your eyes.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Do not expose the product to fire or excessive temperatures. Doing so may result in an explosion if the temperature exceeds the rated limit.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Any use of unrecommended or non\sphinxhyphen{}supplied materials or parts may result in a risk of fire, electric shock, or personal injury.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Do not leave the battery charging unattended for long periods. Always monitor the charging process to ensure safe operation.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
To reduce the risk of electric shock, unplug the product from any power source before attempting any technical service or troubleshooting.

\end{itemize}

\end{safetytwocol}

\safetysubbar{OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS}

\begin{safetytwocol}

\safetylead{SAVE THESE INSTRUCTIONS}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Stop using the product immediately if it shows signs of damage. Discontinue use and contact customer support for assistance.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Do not charge the battery in extremely hot or cold environments and strictly adhere to the specified operating temperature ranges.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Charging temperature: \sphinxhyphen{}4°F to 113°F (\sphinxhyphen{}20°C to 45°C).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Discharging temperature: \sphinxhyphen{}4°F to 113°F (\sphinxhyphen{}20°C to 45°C).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
To ensure proper air circulation, keep the product vents uncovered.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Charging in damp or poorly ventilated spaces may cause safety hazards.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Water can cause short circuits or damage to the charger, leading to safety risks.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Unplug the power cord from a power outlet during a storm.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Immediately turn off the product if it has fallen, been dropped, or exposed to vibrations.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Ensure the device(s) are powered off before connecting them to the product.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Do not charge the product using a damaged or broken charging cord or plug.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Do not use the product to charge any device with a damaged or broken cable or plug.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Always unplug the charging cord by pulling the plug, not the cord, to reduce the risk of damage.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Ensure the product is properly secured when transporting it in a moving vehicle.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
DO NOT place the unit upside down or on its side during use or storage.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
DO NOT place the product on the floor or at a height less than 18 inches (457 mm) above the floor during operation in a workshop or repair facility.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
DO NOT use the product’s accessories with other devices or equipment.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Solar charge time depends on weather conditions. Place your solar panel where it will get as much direct sunlight as possible.

\end{itemize}

\end{safetytwocol}

\HBApplyLang{en}




\section{Danger}
\label{\detokenize{index:danger}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
This device is intended for indoor use only (please place this device in a similar indoor environment when using it outdoors, e.g., home RVs, tents, cabins, etc.).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
This device is not waterproof or dustproof. Keep away from rain and humid environments during use.

\end{itemize}


\section{User Maintenance Instructions}
\label{\detokenize{index:user-maintenance-instructions}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
During the lifecycle of energy storage products, a certain degree of capacity and energy degradation is expected.
As the number of charge and discharge cycles increases and storage time extends, this degradation will gradually intensify,
which is a normal phenomenon consistent with the natural aging of battery cells.

\section{WHAT’S IN THE BOX}
\label{\detokenize{index:what-s-in-the-box}}

\subsection{FCC}
\label{\detokenize{index:fcc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This device complies with Part 15 of the FCC Rules. Operation is subject to the following two conditions:
(1) This device may not cause harmful interference, and
(2) This device must accept any interference received, including interference that may cause undesired operation.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{NOTE:} This equipment has been tested and found to comply with the limits for a Class B digital device,
pursuant to Part 15 of the FCC Rules.
These limits are designed to provide reasonable protection against harmful interference in a residential
installation. This equipment generates, uses, and can radiate radio frequency energy and, if not installed
and used in accordance with the instructions, may cause harmful interference to communications.
However, there is no guarantee that radio interference will not occur in a particular installation.
If this equipment does cause harmful interference to radio or television reception, which can be
determined by turning the equipment off and on, the user is encouraged to try to correct the interference
by one or more of the following measures:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Reorient or relocate the receiving antenna.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Increase the separation between the equipment and receiver.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Connect the equipment into an outlet on a circuit different from that to which the receiver is connected.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Consult the dealer or an experienced radio/TV technician for help.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{MODIFICATION:}
Any changes or modifications not expressly approved by the grantee of this device could void the user’s
authority to operate the device.

Ring animation while charging; ring stays on during discharge.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
17
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Low Battery Indicator
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
On: below 20\%. Blink: below 5\%. Off: normal or charging.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
18
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Remaining Battery Percentage
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Displays remaining battery SOC percentage.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
20
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Fault Code
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Indicates product fault. Refer to Troubleshooting.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
21
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Temperature Indicator
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
High/low temperature protection is active until temperature returns to normal range.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
22
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Energy Saving Mode
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Indicates whether output auto\sphinxhyphen{}off protection mode is enabled.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
23
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Output Power
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Displays output power in watts.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
24
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Remaining Discharge Time
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Displays remaining discharging time.
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{note}{Note:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Replace the LCD placeholder with the final UI icon diagram and keep numbering aligned with this table.
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\HBApplyLang{en}


\section{OPERATIONS}
\label{\detokenize{index:operations}}

\subsection{POWER ON/OFF}
\label{\detokenize{index:power-on-off}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
On: press once.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Off: press and hold for 3 seconds.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Default standby time is 2 hours.
The product automatically shuts down after 2 hours of inactivity when there is no charging or discharging.
The standby time can be configured in the Jackery App.

\sphinxAtStartPar
When Energy Saving Mode is enabled, the product will auto shut down after 12 hours
if AC or DC/USB output is on but the unit is neither charging nor discharging.


\subsection{AC OUTPUT ON/OFF}
\label{\detokenize{index:ac-output-on-off}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Prerequisite: product is powered on.
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
On: press once.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Off: press once.

\end{itemize}


\subsection{DC 12V/USB OUTPUT ON/OFF}
\label{\detokenize{index:dc-12v-usb-output-on-off}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Prerequisite: product is powered on.
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
On: press once.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Off: press once.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{CAUTION}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
USB\sphinxhyphen{}C 100W is a high\sphinxhyphen{}power output port. Ensure connected devices and accessories satisfy safety requirements.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Only connect devices/accessories compliant with IEC/EN/UL 62368\sphinxhyphen{}1 clause requirements (or equivalent standards).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
For maximum 100W output, use an official 5A USB\sphinxhyphen{}C cable.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The product can charge a car battery through the Jackery 12V automobile battery charging cable (sold separately).

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{CAUTION}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The DC 12V port supports 12V car batteries only, not 24V systems.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Do not start the car while charging the battery through the product’s 12V port.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
This function is for emergency use and cannot charge a dead or damaged car battery.

\end{itemize}


\subsection{ENERGY SAVING MODE}
\label{\detokenize{index:energy-saving-mode}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Energy Saving Mode is enabled by default to avoid unnecessary battery drain.
If no device is connected, or if connected load stays below threshold
(AC <= 25W or DC/USB <= 2W) for 12 hours, outputs turn off automatically.

\sphinxAtStartPar
To disable Energy Saving Mode, press and hold AC power button + main POWER button for more than 3 seconds.
When disabled, the mode icon disappears and AC/DC outputs no longer auto shut down by this rule.

\noindent\sphinxincludegraphics[width=320\sphinxpxdimen]{{fig}.png}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{NOTE}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Energy Saving Mode resumes its previous state after power\sphinxhyphen{}on. Manual switching is required if you want to change mode.


\subsection{LED LIGHT ON/OFF}
\label{\detokenize{index:led-light-on-off}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The LED light has two modes: Light mode and SOS mode.
In any mode, press and hold the LED light button to turn off the light.
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Press once: Light mode.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Press again: SOS mode.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Press third time: light off.

\end{itemize}


\subsection{LCD SCREEN}
\label{\detokenize{index:lcd-screen}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Shortly On}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Turn on: press the main POWER button, or when the product is charging.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Turn off: press the main POWER button.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Auto\sphinxhyphen{}off: screen turns off after 2 minutes of inactivity and enters sleep mode.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Steady On (charging/discharging state)}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Turn on: press the main POWER button twice when the product is on.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Turn off: press the main POWER button.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Auto\sphinxhyphen{}off: screen turns off after 2 hours of inactivity.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
You can also set display behavior in the Jackery App.


\subsection{KEY COMBINATION}
\label{\detokenize{index:key-combination}}

\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{46}{100}\X{24}{100}\X{30}{100}}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Buttons
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Operation
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Function
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
Main POWER button + AC power button
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Press and hold for 3s
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Turn on/off Energy Saving Mode.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
Main POWER button + DC/USB power button
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Press and hold for 3s
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Reset Wi\sphinxhyphen{}Fi and Bluetooth.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
DC/USB power button + AC power button
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Press and hold for 1s
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Turn on/off Wi\sphinxhyphen{}Fi and Bluetooth.
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}

\noindent\sphinxincludegraphics[width=360\sphinxpxdimen]{{fig}.png}

\HBApplyLang{en}


\section{UNINTERRUPTIBLE POWER SUPPLY (UPS)}
\label{\detokenize{index:uninterruptible-power-supply-ups}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Connect the product to a wall outlet with the AC charging cable, then press the AC power button and power your appliances at the same time.

\sphinxAtStartPar
An uninterruptible power supply (UPS) is a continual power system that provides automated backup electric power to a load when the mains grid power fails.
In the event of a sudden loss of grid power, the Explorer 2000 switches to stored power within 10 ms to keep appliances running.
In UPS mode, the unit peak output reaches 12A (1440W) before power outages. As simultaneous charging and discharging is enabled in Bypass Mode,
the actual output power is lower than rated output power in this mode, but returns to rated output power during outages.

\noindent\sphinxincludegraphics[width=360\sphinxpxdimen]{{fig}.png}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{CAUTION}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
This product does not support 0 ms switching. Do not connect it to equipment that requires a 0 ms switching power supply, such as data servers or workstations.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Before use, test compatibility with your device multiple times.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Do not connect loads exceeding the maximum output power of the product. Otherwise, overload protection will be triggered.

\end{itemize}

\HBApplyLang{en}


\section{CHARGING}
\label{\detokenize{index:charging}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Green energy first: this product supports solar charging and AC wall charging at the same time.
When AC wall charging and solar charging are enabled together, the product prioritizes solar charging,
and both methods are used to charge the battery at the maximum permissible power.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Fully charge the product before its first use.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{NOTE}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Recommended charging temperature: \sphinxhyphen{}4 F to 113 F (\sphinxhyphen{}20 C to 45 C).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Recommended discharging temperature: \sphinxhyphen{}4 F to 113 F (\sphinxhyphen{}20 C to 45 C).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Operating outside this range may restrict charging and discharging capability, or prevent charging/discharging.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Charging power and battery capacity may vary due to temperature fluctuations.

\end{itemize}


\subsection{CHARGING VIA AC WALL OUTLET}
\label{\detokenize{index:charging-via-ac-wall-outlet}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Connect the AC charging cable to the AC input port of the product and a wall outlet.

\noindent\sphinxincludegraphics[width=360\sphinxpxdimen]{{fig}.png}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{CAUTION}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Make sure the AC charging cable is fully and securely plugged into the AC input port.
An incomplete connection may cause unstable current, overheating, poor contact, or device malfunction.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Emergency Charging Mode}

\sphinxAtStartPar
In this mode, the power station can be rapidly charged through AC charging.
This function can be enabled or disabled in the Jackery App.
When emergency charging mode is active, the circular SOC light blinks faster.

\sphinxAtStartPar
To maximize battery life, charge at normal speed whenever possible.
Use emergency charging mode only when necessary; it is not recommended for long\sphinxhyphen{}term frequent use.

\HBApplyLang{en}


\subsection{CHARGING VIA SOLAR PANELS (SOLD SEPARATELY)}
\label{\detokenize{index:charging-via-solar-panels-sold-separately}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Jackery Explorer 2000 has two DC8020 input ports and is compatible with Jackery solar panels.
If one DC8020 input port needs to connect two solar panels simultaneously,
use a solar panel connector (sold separately, not included as standard).

\noindent\sphinxincludegraphics[width=360\sphinxpxdimen]{{fig}.png}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{CAUTION}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
One DC8020 input port can be connected to at most two solar panels.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Ensure the input voltage for both DC input ports is the same. Different voltages may damage the product.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use the same model and the same number of solar panels when connecting both DC8020 input ports.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Do not charge the product using both a car charger and a solar panel at the same time.
Doing so may blow the car fuse or cause charging failure.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
It is recommended to use Jackery solar panels. Ensure the open\sphinxhyphen{}circuit voltage (Voc)
of the solar panel is within the DC input range (16V\sphinxhyphen{}60V) of the Explorer 2000.
Jackery is not responsible for damage or loss caused by third\sphinxhyphen{}party solar panels.


\subsection{CHARGING VIA A CAR CHARGER (SOLD SEPARATELY)}
\label{\detokenize{index:charging-via-a-car-charger-sold-separately}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This product can be charged using a 12V car charger.
Ensure the car charger and the 12V car power outlet provide a stable connection.

\noindent\sphinxincludegraphics[width=360\sphinxpxdimen]{{fig}.png}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleemphasis{The car charging cable is sold separately.}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{CAUTION}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Start the vehicle before charging the power station.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
If the vehicle is on bumpy roads, do not use the car charger.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Vehicle charging is only for 12V DC vehicles, not 24V DC vehicles.

\end{itemize}

\HBApplyLang{en}


\section{STORAGE}
\label{\detokenize{index:storage}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Store the product in a dry, clean place with proper ventilation.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Storage temperature and humidity:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
1 month: \sphinxhyphen{}4 F to 113 F / \sphinxhyphen{}20 C to 45 C (0\sphinxhyphen{}60\%RH)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
3 months: 32 F to 113 F / 0 C to 45 C (0\sphinxhyphen{}60\%RH)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
12 months: 32 F to 77 F / 0 C to 25 C (0\sphinxhyphen{}60\%RH)

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
If the product is stored for a long period (3 to 6 months) with power depleted, it may become unchargeable.
To maintain battery health, check and recharge the product every three months,
and perform a full charge and discharge cycle at least once every 6 to 12 months.

\HBApplyLang{en}


\section{TROUBLESHOOTING}
\label{\detokenize{index:troubleshooting}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
If any of the following fault codes appear, follow the corrective actions below.
If the fault persists, contact Jackery Customer Support.


\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{14}{100}\X{86}{100}}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Error Code
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Corrective Measures
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
F0
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Restart the product.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
F1
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Restart the product.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
F2
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Restart the product.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
F3
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Restart the product.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
F4
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Connect the product to loads to discharge its battery until the fault disappears.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
F5
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Charge the product via solar panels or an AC wall outlet until the fault disappears.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
F6
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Wait for the grid to normalize before charging via AC wall outlet; check whether intake/exhaust vents are blocked and keep at least 0.66 ft (20 cm) clearance on both sides;
keep the product away from direct sunlight and high ambient temperature; disconnect all loads and let the product idle until the fault disappears; then restart.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
F7
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Remove all DC inputs; check the solar panel open\sphinxhyphen{}circuit voltage (Voc) (maximum allowed DC input is 60V); restart and keep the product idle until the fault disappears.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
F8
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Contact Jackery Customer Support.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
F9
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Remove the load connected to the USB ports and wait until the fault disappears.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
FE
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Contact Jackery Customer Support.
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}

\HBApplyLang{en}

\section{WARRANTY}
\label{\detokenize{index:warranty}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
We only provide our warranty to customers who purchase from the official Jackery website,
Jackery\sphinxhyphen{}branded third\sphinxhyphen{}party platforms, or local authorized dealers.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleemphasis{Warranty period and details may vary according to local laws, regulations, and authorized dealers.}


\subsection{Limited Warranty}
\label{\detokenize{index:limited-warranty}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Jackery Inc. warrants to the original consumer purchaser that the Jackery product will be free from defects
in workmanship and material under normal consumer use during the applicable warranty period identified in
this “Warranty Period” section below, subject to the exclusions set forth below.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This warranty statement sets forth Jackery’s total and exclusive warranty obligation.
We will not assume, nor authorize any person to assume for us, any other liability in connection with the sale of our products.


\subsection{Warranty Period}
\label{\detokenize{index:warranty-period}}

\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{50}{100}\X{50}{100}}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{3 YEARS}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Standard Warranty}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The standard warranty period for Jackery Explorer 2000 is 36 months.
In each case, the warranty period is measured starting on the date of purchase
by the original consumer purchaser.
The sales receipt from the first consumer purchaser, or other reasonable documentary proof,
is required in order to establish the start date of the warranty period.
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{2 YEARS}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Extended Warranty}

\sphinxAtStartPar
To activate the Warranty Extension, you must register your product online
or contact our customer service team at \sphinxhref{mailto:hello@jackery.com}{hello@jackery.com}
to extend the standard warranty runtime.
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}


\subsection{Repair or Replacement}
\label{\detokenize{index:repair-or-replacement}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Jackery will repair or replace (at Jackery’s expense) any Jackery product that fails to operate
during the applicable warranty period due to a defect in workmanship or material.
The repaired or replaced product assumes the remaining warranty of the original date of purchase.


\subsection{Limited to Original Consumer Buyer}
\label{\detokenize{index:limited-to-original-consumer-buyer}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The warranty on Jackery’s product is limited to the original consumer purchaser
and is not transferable to any subsequent owner.


\subsection{Exclusions}
\label{\detokenize{index:exclusions}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Jackery’s warranty does not apply to:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Misused, abused, modified, damaged by accident, or used for anything other than normal consumer use as authorized in Jackery’s current product literature.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Attempted repair by anyone other than an authorized facility.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Any product purchased through an online auction house.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Jackery’s warranty does not apply to the battery cell unless the battery cell is fully charged by you within seven days after you purchase the product and at least once every 6 months thereafter.

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Interpretation Rights}
\label{\detokenize{index:interpretation-rights}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Jackery reserves the right to final interpretation of the above customer after\sphinxhyphen{}sales policy.

\HBApplyLang{en}


\section{APP SETUP}
\label{\detokenize{index:app-setup}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{1. Download the App and Log In}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Search for “Jackery” in Google Play or the App Store to install the App, then register and log in.
You can also scan the product QR code to download and install the App.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{2. Add Device}

\sphinxAtStartPar
2.1 Click the add\sphinxhyphen{}device button in the App.

\sphinxAtStartPar
2.2 Press the POWER button on the device to turn it on.
When the Wi\sphinxhyphen{}Fi and Bluetooth icons flash, the device is in network configuration mode.
Tap “Icon Flashed”, allow nearby\sphinxhyphen{}device access, and enable Bluetooth permission.

\noindent\sphinxincludegraphics[width=320\sphinxpxdimen]{{fig}.png}

\sphinxAtStartPar
2.3 After tapping the searched device icon, the App connects the device through Bluetooth automatically.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{NOTE}

\sphinxAtStartPar
If “the device has been bound” appears:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Ask the device owner to share the device through the App, or

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Press and hold POWER + DC/USB power button for 3 seconds to reset Wi\sphinxhyphen{}Fi and Bluetooth, then bind again.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
2.4 After successful Bluetooth connection, enter the Wi\sphinxhyphen{}Fi password and tap OK.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{NOTE}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use a 2.4 GHz Wi\sphinxhyphen{}Fi network. 5 GHz Wi\sphinxhyphen{}Fi is not supported.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
After successful device addition, the Wi\sphinxhyphen{}Fi icon stays on.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The screenshots in the official manual are for reference only.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{CAUTION}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The Jackery App can connect to only one power station over Bluetooth at a time.
Returning to the device list disconnects Bluetooth automatically.
Tap the power station again to reconnect.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{3. Unbind Device}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Open device Settings from the top\sphinxhyphen{}right corner of the device page,
then tap Unbind at the bottom of the settings page.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{4. Wi\sphinxhyphen{}Fi and Bluetooth Shortcuts}

\sphinxAtStartPar
4.1 Turn on Wi\sphinxhyphen{}Fi and Bluetooth
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
They turn on automatically after device startup.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Or hold DC/USB power button + AC power button until both icons light up.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
4.2 Turn off Wi\sphinxhyphen{}Fi and Bluetooth
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Hold DC/USB power button + AC power button until both icons are off.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
4.3 Reset Wi\sphinxhyphen{}Fi and Bluetooth
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Hold POWER button + DC/USB power button for 3 seconds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The connected App account will be unbound after reset.

\end{itemize}

\noindent\sphinxincludegraphics[width=320\sphinxpxdimen]{{fig}.png}
